intent: Let the PC survive the normal digital-wellbeing auto-shutdown during specific live-event windows (2026 World Cup matches) via self-expiring, friction-gated overrides; surface the exact next-shutdown clock time (or the overridden time) in the i3bar; and make the i3blocks deploy drift-proof so the running scripts can never silently diverge from the repo.
scope: Non-goal: does not weaken the permanent shutdown ratchet or lower MORNING_END_HOUR's hard floor; overrides are time-boxed and expire automatically.
changes: Added a time-boxed override system: shutdown-override-manager.sh (add/list/remove) writes absolute-epoch windows to /etc/shutdown-schedule-overrides.conf; adding requires an exact typed confirmation phrase plus a 30s delay. The generated day-specific-shutdown-check.sh now skips the shutdown decision inside an active window (pruning expired entries) and gained a DRY_RUN guard around rtcwake/poweroff/hibernate for safe testing.

Rewrote the i3blocks shutdown_countdown.sh to show the exact clock time of the next shutdown (e.g. '21:00') instead of a relative countdown, to show the overridden end time in green when an override is active or will rescue the upcoming shutdown, and made the skip-dates check fork-free (bash builtin read loop replacing grep -qxF) so the hot path spawns zero external processes.

Changed install.sh to deploy i3blocks by symlinking each repo file into ~/.config/i3blocks (was cp -r, the source of silent drift), added cd-to-script-dir so it runs from anywhere, and restricted chmod +x to *.sh so the non-executable config file is left at 0644. Reconciled all 18 live files (17 scripts + config) to symlinks.

Repaired the previously-dead i3blocks regression suite: fixed the stale i3-configuration path in both test files, updated the shutdown assertion to the new absolute-time output, added hermetic override coverage, and re-verified the fork-count guarantee (<=1 exec).

verification.command: sudo DRY_RUN=1 /usr/local/bin/day-specific-shutdown-check.sh (with a short test override active)
verification.evidence: journalctl showed 'Active override in effect (reason: ...) - skipping shutdown check' and the script exited without evaluating the day-of-week branch; outside the window it logged the normal skip/shutdown decision unchanged.
Normal time -> '⏻ 21:00'; inside override -> '▶ 00:30 (override)' green; gap between windows -> '⏻ SHUTDOWN'; matching skip-date -> empty output exit 0. Matches expected enforcement.
Exactly 1 execve (the bash interpreter); no grep/date forks in the hot path after the builtin-read rewrite.
Exit code 0 - 'All i3blocks efficiency regression tests passed.' including the new absolute-time and override assertions and the fork-count check.

verification.command: shellcheck on shutdown_countdown.sh and install.sh (-s sh); bash -n on setup_midnight_shutdown.sh
verification.evidence: All lint-clean, zero warnings; setup script syntax OK. Live install.sh run by the user completed through 'i3-msg reload' -> [{"success":true}] and 18/18 files verified as symlinks.

risks: Overrides intentionally suspend the wellbeing shutdown for their window; a mistakenly-long window keeps the PC up. Mitigated by the 12h max duration, 3-window cap, typed-confirmation + 30s delay on add, and automatic expiry.

The override and schedule config files must stay world-readable (0644) because the i3blocks countdown runs as the regular user; a future 0600 regression would blank the block (the script now degrades on unreadable files rather than crashing).
Symlink deploy assumes the repo stays at its current path; moving the repo breaks the ~/.config/i3blocks symlinks until install.sh is re-run.
rollback: i3blocks: git checkout the four repo files, then re-run install.sh (or cp the previous scripts into ~/.config/i3blocks) to restore prior deploy; symlinks are replaced idempotently.
Overrides: sudo shutdown-override-manager.sh remove <n> cancels a window immediately, or truncate /etc/shutdown-schedule-overrides.conf; the check script prunes expired entries on its own.
After rollback, run bash tests/test_i3blocks_efficiency.sh and confirm the i3bar block still renders to validate the restored state.
